Typing Speed Test

Measure your typing speed in WPM. Test how fast and accurately you can type with our free online typing speed test. Track your progress over time.

0WPM
0%Accuracy
0sTime
0Chars

Complete the game to see your score!

How to Take the Typing Test

1

Click the text input area or press any key to begin the test. The timer will start automatically when you begin typing.

2

Type the text exactly as shown. Match all punctuation, capitalization, and spacing as closely as possible.

3

Your WPM score and accuracy percentage will appear when you finish typing the entire passage.

4

Click 'Restart' to try again with a different text passage. Try to beat your previous score!

Tips to Improve Your Score

  • Keep your eyes on the text, not the keyboard — this is the foundation of touch typing
  • Maintain proper posture — sit up straight with your feet flat on the floor
  • Practice daily for at least 15 minutes — consistency beats marathon sessions
  • Focus on accuracy before speed — speed will come naturally with practice
  • Use all ten fingers — proper finger placement on the home row is essential
  • Take short breaks between practice sessions to avoid fatigue
  • Warm up your fingers before starting a typing session
  • Don't rush — typing at a steady, consistent pace is more effective than rushing

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good typing speed?

The average typing speed is around 40 WPM. A good typing speed is 60-80 WPM, which puts you above the average. Professional typists and competitive typists can reach speeds of 100+ WPM. For most office jobs, 50-60 WPM is considered acceptable.

How is WPM calculated?

WPM (Words Per Minute) is calculated by dividing the total number of characters typed correctly by 5 (the average word length in English), then dividing by the time in minutes. Errors are typically deducted from your score. Some tests calculate 'net WPM' by subtracting errors from the total, while others use 'gross WPM' which counts all characters typed.

Can I improve my typing speed?

Yes! Regular practice is the key to improving your typing speed. Focus on accuracy first — speed will follow naturally. Practice for 15-30 minutes daily for the best results. Using proper finger placement (touch typing) and maintaining good posture will also help significantly.

Why is my typing speed different on different tests?

Different typing tests use different text passages with varying difficulty levels. Some texts contain more complex words, punctuation, or capitalization. The length of the test also matters — shorter tests may show higher WPM. For the most accurate measure, take multiple tests and average the results.

Is touch typing worth learning?

Absolutely! Touch typing (typing without looking at the keyboard) can significantly improve your speed and accuracy. It also reduces eye strain and neck pain from constantly looking down at the keyboard. Most people can learn touch typing in 2-3 weeks with consistent practice.

About the Typing Test

The Typing Test is one of our most popular brain training exercises. Designed to test and improve your cognitive abilities, this game provides instant feedback on your performance.

Regular practice with this type of cognitive exercise can help improve your mental processing speed, accuracy, and overall brain health. We recommend practicing for 10-15 minutes daily for the best results.

Your scores are saved locally on your device, allowing you to track your progress over time. Challenge yourself to beat your personal best with each attempt!
